event: mural thickening of the terminal ileum with significantly enlarged mesenteric lymph nodes, mesenteric fat stranding, and intra-abdominal free fluid, suggesting abdominal granulomatous infection or neoplastic process
abnormal CD4/CD8 double-negative T-cell population (38%) with multiple phenotypic aberrancies
high-grade peripheral T-cell lymphoma (PTCL), not other specified
transferred to the medical intensive care unit (ICU)
stayed in ICU care for around 1 week
transferred to the national cancer center
commenced on the EPOCH chemotherapy protocol (etoposide, prednisone, vincristine sulfate [oncovin], cyclophosphamide, and doxorubicin hydrochloride [hydroxydaunorubicin])
attained complete metabolic remission by positron emission tomography/computed tomography
power was decreased in all proximal and distal muscles (3/5)
normal sensory nerve action potential amplitude
needle electromyogram of proximal and distal muscles in the lower and upper limbs
repetitive nerve stimulation (at 30–40 Hz) showed a significant incremental response
started empirically on intravenous immunoglobulins for 5 days
significant improvement of his motor function
planned for consolidation by autologous bone marrow transplant
